message:
chore(gitignore): ignore beads orphan_recovery DB snapshots

The beads daemon's orphan-recovery codepath drops three sibling files
into `.beads/` whenever it heals a corrupted database, named like:

    .beads/beads.db.backup_orphan_recovery_<unix_ts>
    .beads/beads.db.corrupt_orphan_recovery_<unix_ts>
    .beads/beads.db-wal.corrupt_orphan_recovery_<unix_ts>

These are per-machine forensic snapshots — multi-megabyte SQLite
dumps that exist only so the daemon can reconstruct state if the
recovery itself fails. They have no value off the host and the
existing `.beads/*.db*` patterns in `.beads/.gitignore` do not
catch them: the recovery suffix sits *after* the database
extension (so `*.db` only matches files literally ending in
`.db`), and `.beads/beads.db.rebuild_*` / `.beads/beads.db.stale_*`
only match the `rebuild_*`/`stale_*` recovery flavors.

Add two compact patterns under the existing
"# Beads recovery artifacts" block:

    .beads/*.backup_*
    .beads/*.corrupt_*

`*.backup_*` matches `beads.db.backup_orphan_recovery_*`.
`*.corrupt_*` matches both `beads.db.corrupt_orphan_recovery_*`
and `beads.db-wal.corrupt_orphan_recovery_*` because the leading
glob spans the entire `beads.db-wal` filename prefix. The patterns
are deliberately broader than just the `_orphan_recovery_` suffix
so future beads versions that introduce a new recovery flavor
under the same `.backup_` or `.corrupt_` prefix are auto-ignored
without another gitignore round-trip.

Verified locally: `git status` no longer surfaces the three
recovery snapshots that motivated this change, and no
previously-tracked files have suddenly become ignored (the
patterns only match files with a timestamped recovery suffix).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
